descriptions.projects_claim-token_delete-project: Revoke the active project claim token for a Supabase project so pending claim links can no longer transfer ownership. Required: ref (project reference, path param). No request body. Destructive: any unclaimed transfer link backed by the token stops working immediately. Returns 204 No Content on success. Pair with the claim-token create endpoint when you need to rotate the token.
descriptions.projects_config_modify-database-disk: Resize or reconfigure the database disk for a Supabase project (size, type, IOPS, throughput). Required: ref (path) and attributes (request body object — the new disk configuration). Returns 201 with the accepted change record. Use this for upsizing storage or moving between gp3/io2 tiers; the operation can take several minutes to complete and may briefly impact connections.
descriptions.projects_pause_a-project: Pause the given Supabase project so its database, edge functions, and storage stop billing compute. Required: ref (project reference, path param). No request body. Returns 200 once the pause is accepted. Use projects_restore_a-project to bring the project back online; long-paused projects on free tier are eligible for automatic teardown.
descriptions.projects_readonly_get-mode-status: Read the current read-only mode state for a Supabase project's database. Required: ref (path). Returns the ReadOnlyStatusResponse with enabled (auto-engaged when storage is full), override_enabled (operator manual override), and override_active_until (expiry timestamp for the override). Use this before issuing writes to detect storage-full lockouts; pair with the override endpoint when you need a temporary writable window for cleanup.
descriptions.projects_restore_a-project: Resume a paused Supabase project, restoring compute for database, functions, and storage. Required: ref (path). No request body. Returns 200 when the restore is accepted; the database may take several minutes to become reachable. Mirror of projects_pause_a-project. Use projects_restore_cancel-a-project-restoration only while a restore is still in progress.
descriptions.projects_restore_cancel-a-project-restor: Cancel an in-flight restore for a paused Supabase project, reverting it to the paused state. Required: ref (path). No request body. Returns 200 when the cancellation is accepted; has no effect once the restore has already completed. Use only when you triggered projects_restore_a-project in error.
descriptions.supabase-profile_get: Return the authenticated Supabase user's profile (the operator behind the personal access token). Returns V1ProfileResponse with gotrue_id (Supabase auth user id), primary_email, and username. Useful for confirming which account a token belongs to before running destructive cross-project commands.
